sections.paragraph:
(Chald. id., Arab. سَنَّ) to sharpen; a sword, Deu 32:41 metaph. the tongue, i.e. to assail any one with sharp sayings, Psa 64:4 140:4 . Part. pass. שָׁנוּן sharpened (of a weapon), Psa 45:6 Isa 5:28.

Piel, to inculcate any thing on any one (Germ. einfchärfen); followed by acc. of pers. and dat. of thing, Deu 6:7.

Hithpoel, to be wounded, as if pierced through (with grief), Psa 73:21.

Derivatives, שַׁיִת (which see, for שֶׁנֶת ), שְׁנִינָה.
